content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 29, 1988

Louis Martinez
4654 E. 57th Street, Apt. A
Maywood, CA 90270

Re: Your Requests for Advice
Our File Nos. I-88-034 and I-88-038

Dear Mr. Martinez:

We have received your letter regarding the Maywood City
Council's adoption of an item which was not on the posted
agenda and your subsequent letter concerning the council's
consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment
project in your city.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.1/ The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials.

The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by
the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise on
these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct
your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city
council to your county district attorney and the consideration
of the negative declaration to your city attorney.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Jeanette Turvill
Legal Assistant
Legal Division

JET:jaj

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city council to your county district attorney and the consideration of the negative declaration to your city attorney.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC has jurisdiction over issues involving a city council's adoption of agenda items not properly posted and the council's consideration of negative declarations on redevelopment projects.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction as the matters raised do not fall within the Political Reform Act's scope. Requesters should direct their questions to the county district attorney regarding procedural council matters and the city attorney regarding environmental review matters.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Five substantially identical declination letters sent to different Maywood requesters (Louis Martinez, Ronald E. Fick, Donna Mayfield, Pat Quiles, and Maywood Concerned Citizens) regarding file numbers I-88-034 and I-88-038, all dated January 29, 1988. No distinct Q/C sections; conclusion derived from body text. Letters explain FPPC's limited jurisdiction and redirect requesters appropriately.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC has jurisdiction over issues involving a city council's adoption of agenda items not properly posted and the council's consideration of negative declarations on redevelopment projects.

CONCLUSION: The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city council to your county district attorney and the consideration of the negative declaration to your city attorney.

embedding.summary: The FPPC issued form declination letters to five Maywood residents and citizen groups explaining that their inquiries about city council agenda procedures and negative declarations on redevelopment projects fall outside FPPC jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act, and directing them to appropriate alternative authorities.
